channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ghanaian Government Coordinates Evacuation of Nationals from South Africa Following Xenophobic Violence
item.description: More than 800 Ghanaians living in South Africa have registered for evacuation due to a recent surge in xenophobic attacks. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ghana confirmed that the evacuation, origi...

item.title: Arbitrator Rules Air Transat Cabin Crew Prohibited from Using Cannabis On or Off Duty
item.description: An arbitrator has ruled that Air Transat cabin crew members are prohibited from consuming cannabis both during work hours and in their personal time. The decision follows a dispute between the airline...

item.title: Waymo Temporarily Halts Robotaxi Service in Four Cities Due to Flooding Risks
item.description: Waymo has temporarily suspended its robotaxi operations in four U.S. cities—Atlanta, San Antonio, Dallas, and Houston—after its autonomous vehicles struggled with flooded roads during heavy rainfall. ...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Sharif to undertake official visit to China from May 23-26
item.description: According to an official statement from the Foreign Office spokesperson Tahir Andrabi, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif is scheduled to visit China from May 23 to May 26 upon the formal invitation of the...

item.title: Rumuokparali Monarch Warns Community About Attempted Leadership Takeover
item.description: Eze Onyema Dike-Nene, di Paramount Ruler of Rumuokparali Clan for Obio/Akpor LGA, don raise alarm say some people for di community dey try frame am and him family to remove am from di throne. For one ...

item.title: French Open Maintains Prize Money Despite Player Complaints
item.description: The French Open organizers have confirmed that there will be no changes to the tournament’s prize money this year, despite growing dissatisfaction from top tennis players. Tournament director Amelie M...

item.title: US Senators Propose Review of Tanzania Relations and Suspend Aid Pending Democratic Reforms
item.description: On 19th May 2026, U.S. senators Jeanne Shaheen (Democrat) and Ted Cruz (Republican) tabled the bipartisan Reassessing the United States-Tanzania Bilateral Relationship Act. The bill calls for a detail...

item.title: Prasa unions demand urgent talks over retrenchment affecting 600 staff
item.description: The two major unions at the Passenger Rail Agency of South Africa (Prasa), the United National Transport Union (Untu) and the SA Transport and Allied Workers Union (Satawu), have called for urgent tal...

item.title: US Imposes Travel Ban on Tanzanian Police Officer Over Human Rights Concerns
item.description: The United States government has placed travel restrictions on Tanzanian police officer Faustine Mafwele, following credible reports linking him to serious human rights abuses. Secretary of State Marc...

item.title: Kwara PDP Publicity Secretary Remanded Over Alleged Defamation Case
item.description: Olusegun Olushola Adewara, wey be Kwara State Publicity Secretary for Peoples Democratic Party (PDP), don dey remanded for Oke-Kura Correctional Centre. Dis na after Ilorin State High Court reject him...

item.title: Kenya and WHO Seek Stronger Collaboration to Address Health Worker Shortages in Africa
item.description: Kenya, together with the World Health Organization (WHO), is pushing for stronger cooperation with Europe to tackle growing shortages of skilled healthcare workers and the challenges posed by migratio...

item.title: Democratic Party releases report analysing Kamala Harris' 2024 election defeat
item.description: The Democratic National Committee (DNC) has finally released a previously withheld report analysing Vice President Kamala Harris' loss to Donald Trump in the 2024 US presidential election. The 192-pag...

item.title: BuzzFeed Quiz: Discover Your Ideal 'Off Campus' Character Match
item.description: BuzzFeed recently published a lighthearted quiz aimed at fans of the popular webcomic and novel series 'Off Campus.' The quiz, authored by Henry Dansicker, invites readers to discover which male chara...

item.title: The Life of Paul Karason, Known as the 'Blue Man,' and the Effects of Colloidal Silver
item.description: Paul Karason, widely known as the 'Blue Man' or 'Papa Smurf,' became famous for his unusual skin condition caused by long-term use of colloidal silver. In the early 1990s, Karason, who had fair skin w...

item.title: Muzaffarabad college principal killed in shooting incident outside campus
item.description: In a tragic incident in Muzaffarabad, Azad Jammu and Kashmir, the principal of a private college, Hamza Ahmed Dar, succumbed to injuries after being shot outside his college. Police reports indicate t...

item.title: Air India Flight Lands Safely in Delhi After Suspected Engine Fire
item.description: On Thursday evening, May 21, 2026, an Air India flight from Bengaluru to Delhi with 160 passengers on board had to make an emergency landing at Delhi airport due to a suspected engine fire. According ...

item.title: Putco announces 10% fare increase from June amid rising fuel prices
item.description: Commuters in Gauteng are facing steeper travel costs as both Putco buses and minibus taxis prepare for fare increases. Putco has announced a 10% fare hike from 1 June due to rising diesel prices linke...

item.title: Recognising Common Signs of Drug Use in Loved Ones
item.description: Drug use has become increasingly common in the UK, affecting people across all ages and social backgrounds. According to the Crime Survey for England and Wales, 8.7 per cent of people aged 16 to 59 re...

item.title: Flagship Minerals raises A$2.75M to advance Isidora gold project in Chile
item.description: Flagship Minerals has successfully raised A$2.75 million through a strategic placement to accelerate development at its Isidora gold project in Chile. The funding will support key technical workstream...

item.title: Lawyer Criticizes Supreme Court's Use of Purcell Principle in Election Cases
item.description: During a recent episode of his 'Democracy Docket' podcast, attorney Marc Elias criticized the Supreme Court for its inconsistent application of the Purcell Principle in election law cases. Elias descr...
